mechanism_narrative: The level of industrial production indexes overall economic output; when INDPRO is above its 12-month moving average, it indicates expanding manufacturing activity which supports corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of economic health
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of INDPRO relative to its 12-month moving average is below 0.03 over any 3-year window, or if the forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months when INDPRO is above its 12-month MA, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a weakening labor market, reducing consumer income and spending, which depresses corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after each trigger occurrence the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and retail sales growth—where HOUST declines month-over-month for two consecutive months while RSAFS continues to rise—indicates an imbalanced consumer sector; housing weakness foreshadows economic contraction that reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline for 2 consecutive months while RSAFS month-over-month change positive
failure_condition: If in triggered events the average 60-day SPX return is positive, or if the signal has less than 55% accuracy rate across all historical instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an increase in the effective federal funds rate signals further restrictive policy that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 6-month moving average is above 3-month moving average (indicating tightening trend)
failure_condition: If during periods identified as tightening cycles the forward 20-day SPX returns are not statistically significantly lower than in non-tightening periods (p < 0.05 one-tailed), or if the sign of the relationship flips in at least 30% of tightening episodes, reject.

mechanism_narrative: High and rising levels of real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand, which supports near-term corporate revenue and stock prices, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS changes and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is not positive over any 2-year window, or if the strategy trading on RSAFS increases fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 5-day periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP (GDPC1 growth rate below 1.5% annualized) signals an impending recession, causing investors to reduce equity exposure and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If after each trigger the 60-day SPX return is positive more than 40% of the time, or if the average return is not significantly negative at the 95% confidence level, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises for two consecutive months while nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) continue to gain, it signals that the labor market is deteriorating from a healthy state, as discouraged workers re-enter the labor force; this divergence traditionally precedes equity market declines over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases for 2 consecutive months while PAYEMS month-over-month change positive
failure_condition: If the signal produces negative returns less than 60% of the time after trigger, or if the average return is not statistically different from zero (p >= 0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a mid-cycle economic expansion, acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO month-over-month growth above 0.5%) confirms that the supply side of the economy is strengthening, boosting corporate earnings and driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in mid-cycle expansion periods, triggered signals result in average 20-day SPX returns not significantly positive (p > 0.10), or if the strategy underperforms a simple buy-and-hold over 3-year rolling windows, reject.
